vars.TAGLINE: The body's own repair signal, amplified.
vars.BULLET_1: Activates VEGFR2 + Akt/eNOS pathway — drives new blood vessel growth at injury sites.
vars.BULLET_2: Upregulates growth hormone receptor expression in tendon fibroblasts at mRNA + protein level.
vars.BULLET_3: Accelerates fibroblast migration via FAK-paxillin pathway — the cells that lay down collagen scaffolding.

caption:
BPC-157. A pentadecapeptide derived from a protein fragment found naturally in human stomach lining. Why has it become one of the most studied peptides in regenerative research?

Research points to multi-pathway action — angiogenesis, GH receptor upregulation, and accelerated fibroblast migration all from a single compound. A 2025 systematic review of 36 studies (1993-2024) reported consistent healing acceleration across muscle, tendon, ligament, and bone in animal injury models.

Unusually broad. Unusually well-documented.

Full breakdown on the product page →

#peptideresearch #BPC157

vars.TAGLINE: GLP-1 + GIP. One molecule, two receptors.
vars.BULLET_1: Activates both GLP-1 and GIP receptors — first dual agonist in clinical use (Mounjaro, Zepbound).
vars.BULLET_2: Slows gastric emptying while amplifying glucose-dependent insulin secretion in the pancreas.
vars.BULLET_3: Activates satiety circuits in the hypothalamus — research shows up to 22.5% body weight reduction over 72 weeks (SURMOUNT-1 trial).

caption:
Tirzepatide. The first dual GLP-1/GIP receptor agonist in clinical use — the molecule behind Mounjaro and Zepbound.

What makes it different from semaglutide isn't the GLP-1 mechanism (both have it), but the addition of GIP. The two receptors work in concert: GLP-1 slows gastric emptying and amplifies insulin secretion; GIP appears to enhance satiety further while also influencing fat utilization.

The SURMOUNT-1 trial reported up to 22.5% body weight reduction at 72 weeks — among the largest pharmacological weight changes ever published.

Full breakdown on the product page →

#peptideresearch #GLP1 #tirzepatide

vars.TAGLINE: The cellular currency of energy and repair.
vars.BULLET_1: Essential coenzyme for ATP production — depletes with age, stress, and oxidative load.
vars.BULLET_2: Activates sirtuin family proteins (SIRT1, SIRT3) involved in mitochondrial biogenesis and DNA repair.
vars.BULLET_3: Substrate for PARP enzymes that respond to DNA damage and CD38 in immune signaling.

caption:
NAD+ isn't a peptide. It's a coenzyme. But it sits at the center of basically every longevity research conversation for a reason: levels measurably decline with age, and restoring them shifts the activity of sirtuins, PARPs, and mitochondrial repair pathways.

Research has explored NAD+ supplementation through both direct administration and precursor pathways (NMN, NR). Studies indicate restoration of certain age-related metabolic markers in animal models.

Not a panacea. Just one of the most-studied longevity research targets in the past decade.

#NAD #longevityresearch

vars.TAGLINE: Synthetic Thymosin Beta-4. The body's actin organizer.
vars.BULLET_1: Binds G-actin to regulate cytoskeletal organization — fundamental to cell migration and tissue repair.
vars.BULLET_2: Promotes angiogenesis, wound healing, and reduces inflammation in animal models of injury.
vars.BULLET_3: Often paired with BPC-157 in research stacks for synergistic recovery effects.
vars.CITATION_SHORT: Goldstein et al · 2012 · Ann NY Acad Sci 1269(1)

caption:
TB-500 (the synthetic form of Thymosin Beta-4) is one of the most well-characterized recovery peptides in published research.

Its core mechanism is mechanical: it binds G-actin and modulates cytoskeletal organization, which is fundamental to how cells migrate, heal, and repair damaged tissue. Beyond actin binding, research has explored its effects on angiogenesis, hair follicle stem cell activation, and corneal wound healing.

In the recovery research literature, TB-500 is often discussed alongside BPC-157 — different mechanisms, frequently paired.

Full breakdown on the product page →

#TB500 #peptideresearch #recovery

vars.TAGLINE: The molecule that redefined obesity research.
vars.BULLET_1: GLP-1 receptor agonist with extended half-life (~7 days) — once-weekly dosing in clinical protocols.
vars.BULLET_2: Slows gastric emptying, enhances satiety, improves insulin response — multiple mechanisms reinforce each other.
vars.BULLET_3: STEP-1 trial: average 14.9% body weight reduction over 68 weeks vs 2.4% placebo.

caption:
Semaglutide. The molecule behind Ozempic and Wegovy. The compound that recast the entire conversation around obesity research.

Mechanism: GLP-1 receptor agonist. The clinical effect comes from layered actions — slower gastric emptying, central satiety signaling, glucose-dependent insulin release — none of them dramatic alone, but combined and sustained over weeks they produce the trial outcomes that made the headlines.

The STEP-1 trial (NEJM 2021) reported 14.9% mean body weight reduction at 68 weeks vs 2.4% placebo. Not a small effect.

Full breakdown on the product page →

#semaglutide #GLP1 #peptideresearch

vars.TAGLINE: A peptide encoded inside mitochondrial DNA.
vars.BULLET_1: Mitochondrial-derived peptide that improves insulin sensitivity in animal models of metabolic stress.
vars.BULLET_2: Activates AMPK signaling — the cellular energy sensor — to enhance glucose homeostasis.
vars.BULLET_3: Research links MOTS-c to exercise capacity, metabolic flexibility, and age-related decline pathways.

caption:
MOTS-c is unusual: it's a peptide encoded by mitochondrial DNA, not nuclear DNA. Discovered in 2015. Functions as a hormone-like signal across tissues.

Mechanism research centers on AMPK activation — the cellular fuel-gauge enzyme — which downstream improves glucose uptake and metabolic flexibility. Animal model studies show enhanced insulin sensitivity and improved exercise capacity.

Because mitochondrial decline tracks closely with aging, MOTS-c has become a focus of longevity research. Still early-stage; limited human data.

Full breakdown on the product page →

#MOTSc #mitochondrial #longevityresearch

vars.TAGLINE: GHRH analog. Triggers your pituitary to do its own work.
vars.BULLET_1: 29-amino-acid GHRH analog — same pulsatile mechanism as endogenous GH-releasing hormone.
vars.BULLET_2: Stimulates pituitary GH release rather than supplying exogenous GH.
vars.BULLET_3: Research has explored applications in age-related GH decline, sleep architecture, and recovery markers.

caption:
Sermorelin is a GHRH (growth hormone releasing hormone) analog — the first 29 amino acids of endogenous GHRH, which is the bioactive segment.

The mechanism is indirect: rather than supplying GH from the outside, sermorelin signals the pituitary to release its own. Research suggests this preserves the natural pulsatile pattern of GH secretion — which differs meaningfully from continuous exogenous GH administration.

Applications in published research span age-related GH decline studies, sleep architecture, and post-injury recovery markers.

Full breakdown on the product page →

#sermorelin #GHRH #peptideresearch

vars.TAGLINE: The C-terminal of α-MSH. Anti-inflammatory in three letters.
vars.BULLET_1: Tripeptide (Lysine-Proline-Valine) — the active C-terminal fragment of alpha-melanocyte-stimulating hormone.
vars.BULLET_2: Research shows downregulation of NF-κB signaling — a master regulator of inflammation pathways.
vars.BULLET_3: Animal model studies explore applications in IBD, skin healing, and inflammatory bowel research.

caption:
KPV is small: just three amino acids — Lysine, Proline, Valine. It's the C-terminal tripeptide of alpha-MSH, and it carries much of the anti-inflammatory activity of its parent.

Mechanism research centers on NF-κB pathway downregulation. NF-κB is one of the most upstream inflammation regulators in the cell, so modulating it has broad downstream effects across cytokine production and immune signaling.

Most-published research applications: inflammatory bowel disease models, skin healing, and gut-barrier studies. Small, simple, surprisingly versatile.

Full breakdown on the product page →

#KPV #aMSH #peptideresearch

From the published BPC-157 literature.

Staresinic et al, 2003. One of the foundational papers in the BPC-157 ligament-healing line of research. A dose-dependent effect — the kind of clean signal that gets a research compound taken seriously.

The paper is open-access. Worth a read if you're interested in the original animal model work that built the case for what's now one of the most-studied recovery peptides on the planet.

vars.EXPLAINER_TEXT: NAD+ serves as the substrate for sirtuin deacetylases, which regulate mitochondrial biogenesis, DNA repair, and metabolic adaptation in published longevity research.
vars.DIAGRAM_SVG: <svg viewBox="0 0 400 200" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><defs><marker id="arr" markerWidth="8" markerHeight="8" refX="6" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M0,0 L6,3 L0,6" fill="#00A0A8"/></marker></defs><circle cx="60" cy="100" r="32" fill="none" stroke="#00A0A8" stroke-width="2"/><text x="60" y="105" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Barlow Condensed" font-weight="700" font-size="13" fill="#1A1C22">NAD+</text><line x1="92" y1="100" x2="148" y2="100" stroke="#00A0A8" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arr)"/><circle cx="180" cy="100" r="32" fill="none" stroke="#00A0A8" stroke-width="2"/><text x="180" y="105" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Barlow Condensed" font-weight="700" font-size="11" fill="#1A1C22">SIRT1/3</text><line x1="212" y1="100" x2="268" y2="100" stroke="#E07C24" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arr)"/><circle cx="300" cy="100" r="42" fill="none" stroke="#E07C24" stroke-width="2"/><text x="300" y="100" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Barlow Condensed" font-weight="700" font-size="10" fill="#1A1C22">DNA Repair</text><text x="300" y="115" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Barlow Condensed" font-weight="400" font-size="8" fill="#7A7D88">Mito biogenesis</text></svg>

caption:
The NAD+/sirtuin pathway. The molecular machinery at the center of most longevity research.

NAD+ is the substrate. Sirtuins (SIRT1, SIRT3) are the enzymes that consume it. Downstream: mitochondrial biogenesis, DNA repair via PARP enzymes, metabolic adaptation under stress.

NAD+ levels measurably decline with age. That's the foundation of the entire NAD-restoration line of research.

#NAD #sirtuins #longevityresearch

The single most consequential weight-loss pharmacology paper of the past decade. Worth understanding if you're following the GLP-1 research conversation.

vars.EXPLAINER_TEXT: MOTS-c, encoded in mitochondrial DNA, activates AMPK signaling — improving glucose uptake, metabolic flexibility, and exercise capacity in published research.
vars.DIAGRAM_SVG: <svg viewBox="0 0 400 200" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><defs><marker id="arr2" markerWidth="8" markerHeight="8" refX="6" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M0,0 L6,3 L0,6" fill="#00A0A8"/></marker></defs><ellipse cx="60" cy="100" rx="40" ry="26" fill="none" stroke="#E07C24" stroke-width="2"/><text x="60" y="98" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Barlow Condensed" font-weight="700" font-size="11" fill="#1A1C22">MITO-DNA</text><text x="60" y="112" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Barlow Condensed" font-weight="400" font-size="9" fill="#7A7D88">MOTS-c</text><line x1="100" y1="100" x2="148" y2="100" stroke="#00A0A8" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arr2)"/><circle cx="180" cy="100" r="30" fill="none" stroke="#00A0A8" stroke-width="2"/><text x="180" y="105" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Barlow Condensed" font-weight="700" font-size="13" fill="#1A1C22">AMPK</text><line x1="210" y1="100" x2="258" y2="100" stroke="#00A0A8" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arr2)"/><rect x="262" y="74" width="80" height="52" fill="none" stroke="#E07C24" stroke-width="2" rx="4"/><text x="302" y="96" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Barlow Condensed" font-weight="700" font-size="10" fill="#1A1C22">Glucose uptake</text><text x="302" y="112" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Barlow Condensed" font-weight="400" font-size="9" fill="#7A7D88">Metabolic flex</text></svg>

caption:
The MOTS-c → AMPK pathway. One of the cleanest stories in metabolic research.

MOTS-c is encoded inside mitochondrial DNA — unusual. Discovered in 2015. Activates AMPK, the cellular fuel-gauge. Downstream: improved glucose uptake, metabolic flexibility, enhanced exercise capacity in animal models.

MOTS-c sits at the intersection of mitochondrial biology and exercise physiology — both increasingly important to longevity research.

#MOTSc #AMPK #mitochondrial

vars.QUOTE: LL-37 functions at the interface of innate and adaptive immunity, with broad-spectrum antimicrobial activity and immune-modulating signaling.
vars.CITATION_FULL: Vandamme et al · Cell Mol Immunol · 2012

caption:
From the LL-37 / cathelicidin research literature.

LL-37 is one of the most studied antimicrobial peptides. Endogenous to humans (encoded by the CAMP gene), it sits at the interface of innate and adaptive immunity — direct antimicrobial activity AND broader immune signaling.

Research applications span dermatology, gut health, and host-defense studies. A small molecule with surprisingly wide reach in the literature.

#LL37 #cathelicidin #peptideresearch
